# Sarah's T.A.S.K.

> Sarah's Tactically Aligned Speech Know-How's is a private speech-language pathology practice based in Central Florida, providing personalized, evidence-based speech therapy to children and adults — both in person and virtually across six states (Florida, New York, California, North Carolina, Missouri, and Virginia).

The practice is owned and operated by **Sarah Bipath, M.S., CCC-SLP, CBS** — a board-certified speech-language pathologist with experience across schools, hospitals, skilled nursing facilities, early intervention, outpatient clinics, home health, and private practice. Originally from New York City and now based in Central Florida, Sarah brings advanced training across multiple specialties to deliver care tailored to each client's goals, lifestyle, and stage of life.

The practice's motto: *"Compassion in our hearts, you in our minds."*

## Practice Information

- **Practice name:** Sarah's T.A.S.K. (Tactically Aligned Speech Know-How's)
- **Founder & SLP:** Sarah Bipath, M.S., CCC-SLP, CBS
- **Type:** Private speech-language pathology practice
- **Founded:** Private practice serving Central Florida and virtual clients
- **Phone:** 407-450-3418
- **Email:** admin@sarahstask.com
- **Website:** https://www.sarahstask.com
- **Booking:** https://sarah-bipath.clientsecure.me (Jane App)

## Service Locations

**In-person:** Saint Cloud, FL · Maitland, FL · Surrounding Central Florida (Orange, Osceola, and Seminole counties)

**Virtual (HIPAA-compliant teletherapy, statewide coverage):**
- Florida
- New York
- California
- North Carolina
- Missouri
- Virginia

## Services Offered

### Pediatric Speech Therapy
- Language Delays / Late Language Emergence
- Speech Sound Disorders (Articulation & Phonology)
- Social Communication Skills (Pragmatics)
- Pediatric Feeding & Swallowing
- PROMPT Therapy (tactile-kinesthetic articulation treatment)

### Adult Speech Therapy
- Cognitive-Communication Disorders (post-stroke, TBI recovery, memory strategies)
- Voice Disorders (vocal health, voice therapy, professional voice)
- Accent Reduction (pronunciation, intonation, professional communication)
- Aphasia & Apraxia of Speech (neurological recovery)

### Specialty Services
- **Orofacial Myofunctional Therapy (OMT):** Targeted exercises and behavioral support addressing root causes of persistent speech, feeding, and breathing challenges
- **Craniosacral Fascial Therapy (CFT):** The Gillespie Approach — gently unwinds accumulated fascial strain to support optimal mobility and nervous system regulation
- **Lactation Services:** Expert support for breastfeeding goals including positioning, milk expression, and feeding duration

## Credentials & Training

- **CCC-SLP** — ASHA Certified Speech-Language Pathologist (American Speech-Language-Hearing Association)
- **CBS** — Certified Breastfeeding Specialist
- **PROMPT Trained** — Tactile-kinesthetic speech sound disorder treatment
- **CFT** — Craniosacral Fascial Therapist (Gillespie Approach)
- **Orofacial Myologist** — OMD treatment across the lifespan
- **TOTS Trained** — Tethered Oral Tissues assessment and treatment (tongue, lip, and buccal ties)

## Insurance & Payment

Sarah's T.A.S.K. is a **private-pay practice**. Superbills are provided for clients to submit to insurance for potential out-of-network reimbursement. The practice is also approved through the **Step Up For Students** scholarship program for eligible Florida families.

## Getting Started

New clients begin with a **free 15-minute consultation** to discuss goals, ask questions, and determine fit. The intake and booking process runs through Jane App at https://sarah-bipath.clientsecure.me.

The full intake-to-care process:
1. Free 15-minute consultation
2. Comprehensive evaluation
3. Personalized treatment plan
4. Ongoing sessions with progress monitoring and family collaboration

## Session Details

- **Length:** Most sessions are 30–60 minutes, depending on age, attention span, and treatment plan
- **Ages served:** Infants through seniors — the full lifespan
- **Virtual platform:** HIPAA-compliant video, accessible from any device with a camera and stable internet

## Children's Book — "Where is Zee?"

Sarah is also the author of *"Where is Zee?"*, a heartfelt children's book written in honor of her son's first birthday. As a speech-language pathologist, she views the book as a celebration of emergent literacy — the period from birth onward when language and reading skills first take root.

- **Title:** Where is Zee?
- **Author:** Sarah Bipath, M.S., CCC-SLP
- **Illustrator:** Lola Usupova
- **Available:** Amazon (https://a.co/d/9w7f9F6)
- **Ideal for:** New parents, baby showers, bedtime reading, families building early literacy habits

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://www.sarahstask.com/): Practice overview, services preview, brief FAQs
- [About Sarah](https://www.sarahstask.com/about.html): Bio, credentials, experience across clinical settings, mission and motto

- [FAQ](https://www.sarahstask.com/faq.html): Comprehensive answers across getting started, insurance, sessions, pediatric, adult, specialty services, and virtual care
- [Contact](https://www.sarahstask.com/contact.html): Booking, phone, email, location info, and Jane App intake
- [Where is Zee?](https://www.sarahstask.com/zee.html): Sarah's children's book — story, inspiration, and purchase link
